contaminant.name: Silver Plating Residue
contaminant.description: Tarnish forms as contamination on silver-plating when exposure to air and moisture reacts with sulfur compounds, so layer builds up slowly over time and darkens surface unevenly while losing shine. This contamination exhibits soft and adhesive nature that clings tightly to surface, so it distinguishes from harder oxide scales on other contaminants because flaking occurs less during handling, and on copper tarnish spreads quickly to turn greenish, on brass it mats golden tone without deep penetration, while on bronze patchy blackening appears due to alloy differences. Key challenges arise for removal since scrubbing risks scratching thin plating and chemicals etch underlying layers, so traditional methods fail to preserve shine, but laser cleaning works effectively because pulses vaporize tarnish precisely without heat damage to base and clean surfaces restore uniformly after treatment.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Silver Oxide (Ag2O) and Silver Nanoparticles generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
